page: Microsoft Azure F48ams_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Famsv6 family. It features 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz, with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% tier for stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as well as multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is also in the top 10% tier, while large block read and write bandwidths fall into the top 25% tier. For LLM inference, the server achieves top 10% performance in text generation with medium-sized models, though performance drops to average levels when processing larger 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F48ams_v6 offers high resource density with dedicated physical cores and a substantial memory footprint, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. However, the lack of local storage requires external storage provisioning, which may introduce latency tradeoffs depending on the network configuration. This server is suited for memory-intensive databases, software compilation, medium-scale LLM inference, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure F48ams_v6 is an x86_64 memory-intensive server from the Famsv6 family, configured with 48 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory ratio, but includes no local storage or GPUs. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the server delivers top-tier CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng benchmarks, as well as software compilation. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is also in the top tier, while large block operations fall in the top 25%. The lack of local storage represents a key architectural tradeoff, requiring external storage attachment. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive databases, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F48ams_v6 is a memory-intensive server featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB of RAM, and top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth performance for demanding enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive server featuring forty-eight dedicated AMD EPYC cores and top-tier multi-threaded processing power for enterprise workloads.

384.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
